certificate.moduleName: Samsung NVMe TCG Opal SSC SEDs PM1743 Series
certificate.description: Samsung NVMe TCG Opal SSC SEDs PM1743 Series is a high-performance Self-Encrypting SSDs supporting NVMe Interface that provides AES256-XTS on-the-fly encryption/decryption of user data without performance loss. It implements FIPS approved algorithms for providing cryptographic services such as user data encryption, key protection, FW authentication, and key generation.

approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.name: AES-ECB / FIPS 197, SP 800-38A
approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.useFunction: Approved Block Cipher for Counter DRBG (Cert.# A2248)

approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.name: AES-GCM/ FIPS 197, SP 800-38D
approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.keySize: 256-bit keys with 256-bit key strength IV: 96 bits

approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.name: Counter DRBG / SP 800-90A Rev. 1
approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.keySize: AES 256 bits with Derivation Function Enabled

approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.name: SHA2-384 / FIPS 180-4
approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.useFunction: Message Digest for digital signature verification (Cert.# A2255)

approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.name: CKG / SP 800-133rev2 (Section 4, 6.1, 6.3)
approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.useFunction: Symmetric Cryptographic Key Generation; SP 800- 133rev2 and IG D.H.

approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.name: ENT (P) / SP800-90B
approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.useFunction: ENT (P) provides a minimum of 256 bits of entropy for DRBG seed materials in key generation.

approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.modeMethod: No Security Claimed; AES-GCM is only used for obfuscation and removal of obfuscation the CSP. (IG 2.4.A Scenario #1)
approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.name: AES-GCM / FIPS 197, SP800-38D (non-compliant)

approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.name: The entropy source provides an estimated min-entropy output of 0.58 bits per bit. The DRBG is seeded with 768 bits of entropy input data from the entropy source. Therefore, the DRBG is seeded with at least 384-bits of entropy before generating SSPs.

selfTests.details: Firmware integrity test for Boot Loader is performed by using ECDSA with SHA2-384 at every power-on-reset.
selfTests.name: ECDSA

selfTests.details: Firmware integrity test for the Main Firmware is performed by using ECDSA with SHA2-384 at every power-on-reset.
selfTests.name: ECDSA

selfTests.details: Duplicate Key Test for AES-XTS described in FIPS 140-3 IG C.I (i.e. key_1 ≠ key_2)
selfTests.name: AES-XTS

selfTests.details: KAT: Curve P-384 with SHA-384 signature verification is performed
selfTests.name: ECDSA

selfTests.details: KATs: SP 800-90A Rev. 1 Health testing on Instantiate, Generate and Reseed functions
selfTests.name: CTR DRBG

selfTests.details: KAT: DRBG with AES-256 is performed
selfTests.name: CTR DRBG

selfTests.details: ECDSA signature verification is performed if new FW is downloaded or at every power-on-reset
selfTests.name: ECDSA

selfTests.details: Conditional SP800-90B Health tests: Repetition count test, Adaptive proportion test
selfTests.name: ENT (P)

The entropy source provides an estimated min-entropy output of 0.58 bits per bit. The DRBG is seeded with 768 bits of entropy input data from the entropy source. Therefore, the DRBG is seeded with at least 384-bits of entropy before generating SSPs.
